name: Sleep Disruption
definition: Sleep disruption describes insufficient, irregular, or poor-quality sleep patterns that can influence metabolic, neurocognitive, and immune pathways in population studies. GENARCH includes sleep as an educational exposure domain relevant to gene–environment interaction discussions.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Sleep patterns relate to cognition and neuroinflammatory pathways in population evidence; causal direction is complex.
Sleep disruption is associated with insulin sensitivity and appetite regulation pathways in cohorts.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Circadian shifts and school schedules can shape sleep patterns during adolescence; cohort evidence often evaluates this window.

references.title: Sleep duration and health outcomes: overview of population evidence (review)
